title: conopodium majus subsp. ramosum
intro: Conopodium majus subsp. ramosum, also known as lesser hawkbit or wild chamomile, is a flowering plant species within the dandelion family that is native to Europe, North Africa, and parts of Western Asia. Its medicinal properties include being used as an anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic agent, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain, and combat infections. The health benefits of Conopodium majus subsp. ramosum include alleviating menstrual cramps, calming the nervous system, and promoting relaxation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for stress relief and insomnia. Herbal preparations of Conopodium majus subsp. ramosum can be consumed as a tea, infused oil, or tincture, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
